response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong web/backend experience across TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but the role specifically requires a C++ developer with 8-12 years of hands-on C++, STL, and industrial automation experience. C++ exposure is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and an academic project, with no evidence of message brokers, MQTT, IEC 62443, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.). The profile is a poor match for a senior C++/industrial-automation role despite overall engineering seniority.
strengths: 12+ years of professional software engineering experience with strong craftsmanship/TDD background
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on LabWindows/CVI
Multi-threaded, low-latency, and networked systems experience (DLang at Sociomantic)
concerns: Minimal recent C++ experience; primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang
No demonstrated experience with IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No exposure to industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT, IEC 61850/104, DNP 3.0) or message-broker architectures

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / IEC 62443 / SDL background
Strong general software engineering seniority and systems experience
